title: Manikarnikeswarar Temple
tagline: Manikarnikeswarar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Manikarnikeswarar Temple is a Hindu temple located in the town of Thanjavur, India.
overview.description: Manikarnikeswarar Temple is a Hindu temple located in the town of Thanjavur, India. The temple was constructed by Serfoji II in 1827. The principal deity is Manikarnikeswarar and the goddess, Mangala Nayaki Amman.

topPlaces.name: Thanjavur
topPlaces.description: Thanjavur (Tamil: [t̪aɲdʑaːʋuːɾ]), also known as Thanjai, previously known as Tanjore, is a city in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is the 12th biggest city in Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Achampatti
topPlaces.description: Achampatti is a village in the Budhalur taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Inathukanpatti
topPlaces.description: Inathukanpatti, also known as Inayathukkanpatti, is a village in the Thanjavur taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Indalur
topPlaces.description: Indalur is a village in the Thanjavur taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Saraswathi Mahal Library
topPlaces.description: Saraswathi Mahal Library, also called Thanjavur Maharaja Serfoji's Saraswathi Mahal Library is a library located in Thanjavur (Tanjore), Tamil Nadu, India. It is one of the oldest subsisting libraries in Asia established during 16th century by the Nayak kings of Thanjavur and has on display a rare collection of Palm leaf manuscripts and paper written in Tamil and Sanskrit and a few other indigenous languages of india (especially southern regional languages).

topPlaces.name: Great Living Chola Temples
topPlaces.description: The Great Living Chola Temples is a UNESCO World Heritage Site designation for a group of three Chola dynasty era Hindu temples in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The temple at Thanjavur was added in the list in 1987, while the other two temples were added in 2004.

topPlaces.name: Brihadeeswarar temple fire
topPlaces.description: A fire accident occurred during the consecration of the Brihadeeswarar Temple on 7 June 1997 in Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu, India. A fire cracker lit near the temple fell on the yajnashala, a temporary structure built to accommodate the ritual ceremonies, and sparked the fire that spread to the thatched roofs.

topPlaces.name: Brihadisvara Temple
topPlaces.description: Brihadisvara Temple, called Rajarajesvaram (lit. 'Lord of Rajaraja') by its builder, and known locally as Thanjai Periya Kovil (lit. 'Thanjavur Big Temple') and Peruvudaiyar Kovil (lit. 'Temple of the great lord'), is a Shaivite Hindu temple built in a Chola architectural style located on the south bank of the Cauvery river in Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu, India. It is one of the largest Hindu temples and an exemplar of Tamil architecture.

items.note: Thanjavur (Tamil: [t̪aɲdʑaːʋuːɾ]), also known as Thanjai, previously known as Tanjore, is a city in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is the 12th biggest city in Tamil Nadu.
Achampatti is a village in the Budhalur taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.
Inathukanpatti, also known as Inayathukkanpatti, is a village in the Thanjavur taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.
Indalur is a village in the Thanjavur taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

Saraswathi Mahal Library, also called Thanjavur Maharaja Serfoji's Saraswathi Mahal Library is a library located in Thanjavur (Tanjore), Tamil Nadu, India. It is one of the oldest subsisting libraries in Asia established during 16th century by the Nayak kings of Thanjavur and has on display a rare collection of Palm leaf manuscripts and paper written in Tamil and Sanskrit and a few other indigenous languages of india (especially southern regional languages).

The Great Living Chola Temples is a UNESCO World Heritage Site designation for a group of three Chola dynasty era Hindu temples in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The temple at Thanjavur was added in the list in 1987, while the other two temples were added in 2004.

A fire accident occurred during the consecration of the Brihadeeswarar Temple on 7 June 1997 in Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu, India. A fire cracker lit near the temple fell on the yajnashala, a temporary structure built to accommodate the ritual ceremonies, and sparked the fire that spread to the thatched roofs.

Brihadisvara Temple, called Rajarajesvaram (lit. 'Lord of Rajaraja') by its builder, and known locally as Thanjai Periya Kovil (lit. 'Thanjavur Big Temple') and Peruvudaiyar Kovil (lit. 'Temple of the great lord'), is a Shaivite Hindu temple built in a Chola architectural style located on the south bank of the Cauvery river in Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu, India. It is one of the largest Hindu temples and an exemplar of Tamil architecture.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Manikarnikeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Manikarnikeswarar Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Manikarnikeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (47 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (49 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Manikarnikeswarar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Manikarnikeswarar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Manikarnikeswarar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Manikarnikeswarar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Manikarnikeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thanjavur, Achampatti, Inathukanpatti, Indalur, Saraswathi Mahal Library.
